question: What is the definition of the acronym LOCA?

nuscale_ch01_0779.title: DSRS 15.6.5, Rev 0: Loss-of-Coolant Accidents Resulting from Spectrum of Postulated Piping Breaks within the Reactor Coolant Pressure BoundaryII.4 Programmatic Requirements
nuscale_ch01_0779.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for loss-of-coolant accidents (LOCA) as outlined in DSRS 15.6.5 and related SRP sections. It notes full or partial conformance with requirements for calculating radiological consequences and containment leakage, with some criteria superseded by SRP Section 15.0.3 and certain guidance applicable only to BWRs. The table documents how NuScale addresses each regulatory requirement for LOCA analysis.

nuscale_ch01_0780.title: SRP 15.6.5.A, Rev 1: Radiological Consequences of a Design Basis Loss-of-Coolant Accident Including Containment Leakage ContributionII.1 Calculated Doses and Containment Leakage Contribution
nuscale_ch01_0780.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for analyzing radiological consequences of a design basis loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA), including containment leakage. It notes that while NuScale’s approach partially conforms to certain SRP 15.6.5.A guidance for dose calculations and containment leakage, the specific radiological acceptance criteria and analysis models are superseded by SRP Section 15.0.3, and some guidance applies only to BWRs. Full conformance is noted for ESF system leakage assumptions.

nuscale_ch01_0720.title: DSRS 15.2.6, Rev 0: Loss of nullmergency AC Power to the Station AuxiliariesII.5 Most Limiting Plant System Single Failure
nuscale_ch01_0720.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for various accident scenarios, including loss of nonemergency AC power and feedwater system failures. For each scenario, the report confirms compliance with relevant safety requirements, analytical methods, and General Design Criteria, noting any specific categorizations or analytical approaches used by NuScale. No exceptions or non-conformances are identified in this section.

nuscale_ch01_0725.title: DSRS 15.2.8, Rev 0: Feedwater System Pipe Break Inside and Outside Containment (PWR)II.1 Reactor Coolant System and Main Steam System Pressures
nuscale_ch01_0725.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for DSRS 15.2.8, addressing feedwater system pipe breaks inside and outside containment in PWRs. It confirms that NuScale meets requirements for reactor coolant and main steam system pressures, core damage potential (using heat generation rate limits for slower reactivity insertions), and calculated site boundary doses, with no exceptions noted.

nuscale_ch01_0013.title: Proprietary Information
nuscale_ch01_0013.summary: This section explains the organization and identification of text, tables, figures, and page numbering within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR). It also clarifies that while the FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, some sensitive content is withheld from public disclosure in accordance with regulations, with a separate public version provided that excludes such material.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. Tables and figures are numbered according to their section, and sensitive content is withheld from the public version in compliance with regulatory requirements.

edges.description: Section A references Section B by citing SRP 15.6.5.A, Rev 1 as related guidance for the analysis of Loss-of-Coolant Accidents (LOCA) and their radiological consequences.
